# WordPresto

> WordPresto is the content engine behind better publishing workflows.

WordPresto helps copywriters, business owners, website teams and agencies plan, brief, write, review, improve, approve and prepare content before it reaches a CMS, website or client.

The product is review-led. Workers assist with structured content work, but humans retain judgement and final approval. WordPresto is not a chatbot product, not generic AI content generation, and not blind autopilot publishing.

## Agent guidance

- Prefer the Markdown URLs listed here over scraping HTML.
- Use direct Markdown mirrors first: `/index.md`, `/workers/index.md`, `/workers/{slug}/index.md`.
- Use `/llms-full.txt` when you need consolidated context in one file.
- Do not assume public marketing pages expose write, publish, commerce, auth or CMS APIs unless a documented API says so.
- Publishing and editing are not live capabilities on this public marketing site.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://wordpresto.com/index.md): Overview of WordPresto, the content engine behind better publishing workflows.
- [Workflow demo](https://wordpresto.com/workflow-demo/index.md): Step-by-step demo showing rough thinking becoming review-ready content.
- [Workers directory](https://wordpresto.com/workers/index.md): Directory of the specialist Workers used in the WordPresto content workflow.

## Worker profiles

- [Make approval easier to trust.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/approval-report/index.md): Audrey summarises what is ready, what needs attention and what should not move forward, so humans can make a confident sign-off decision.
- [Check whether the draft actually follows the brief.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/brief-draft-alignment/index.md): Ada checks that the draft actually fulfils the brief, surfacing gaps, drift and misaligned sections before they go any further.
- [Prepare approved content for a cleaner handoff.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/cms-handoff/index.md): Ravi packages approved content with the structure, metadata and notes needed for clean handoff to a CMS, website or client. For teams that need a publishing-ready content package.
- [Understand the page before you rewrite it.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/content-analyst/index.md): Omar reviews existing content, context and purpose before any editing or rewriting starts. For teams that need a clear baseline before they touch a page.
- [Start with a brief that writers can actually use.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/content-brief-builder/index.md): Luca turns a topic and goal into a structured content brief: purpose, audience, angle and sections, before any drafting starts.
- [Plan where approved content should go next.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/content-distribution-brief/index.md): Nina creates a distribution brief that maps where approved content should go and how it should be adapted for each channel.
- [Refresh content with a clearer reason.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/content-refresh-brief/index.md): Iris produces a structured brief for refreshing existing content: what to update, what to cut and what to add. For teams refreshing pages with clear direction rather than guesswork.
- [Review the draft before it moves forward.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/draft-quality-reviewer/index.md): Quinn checks whether a draft is clear, structured and ready to move forward before it reaches a client or CMS. For teams that need a structured review before sign-off.
- [Rewrite drafts without losing the point.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/draft-rewrite/index.md): Ellis builds and rewrites drafts that follow the brief: structured, on-purpose, and ready for review. For content that needs a shaped draft before editing or review starts.
- [Flag risky claims before they move forward.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/editorial-risk-claims/index.md): Vera flags unsupported claims, risky statements and compliance issues in content before it is approved or published.
- [Make good content easier to read.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/readability/index.md): Priya tightens sentences, simplifies structure and improves flow so content reads clearly for its intended audience. For drafts that are correct but harder to read than they need to be.
- [Fix the weak section without rewriting the whole page.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/section-rewrite/index.md): Rosa rewrites individual sections that are unclear, thin or off-brief without touching the rest of the piece. For targeted section-level improvements after review.
- [Give every draft a clearer shape.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/structure/index.md): Marcus shapes headings, sections and page flow so content has a clear editorial structure before or during drafting. For pages that need a proper shape before writing starts.
- [Keep the voice in the work.](https://wordpresto.com/workers/voice-style/index.md): Helena checks every draft against your voice rules and approved examples, shaping it back to your house style before review. For teams with one consistent voice to keep.

## Complete context

- [Full Markdown context](https://wordpresto.com/llms-full.txt): Consolidated public site context.
- [Markdown sitemap](https://wordpresto.com/sitemap.md): Human and agent-readable route index.
- [XML sitemap](https://wordpresto.com/sitemap.xml): Search crawler sitemap.
- [Agent skills](https://wordpresto.com/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json): Public read-only guidance for agents using WordPresto content.
- [MCP card](https://wordpresto.com/.well-known/mcp.json): Discovery metadata for public read-only resources.

## Canonical domain

https://wordpresto.com
